name: Prr South Fork
description_short: Prr South Fork is a federally chartered credit union in Pennsylvania offering savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans to members since 1936.
description_long:
Prr South Fork Federal Credit Union was established in 1936 and is headquartered in South Fork, Pennsylvania. As a small, community-focused financial institution, it has grown to approximately $47.19 million in assets with $37.15 million in deposits and $20.82 million in loans outstanding. The institution is NCUA insured and federally chartered under charter number 909, providing members with federal deposit protection.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of banking products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 1 to 5 years, mortgages for both home purchases and refinancing, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), auto loans, personal loans, and home equity loans. Based on available rate information, they offer competitive products including a 1-year CD at 4.59% APY, a 5-year CD at 4.00% APY, and a 30-year mortgage at 4.91% APR.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Prr South Fork operates on a member-owned cooperative model, which typically results in lower fees and rates more favorable to consumers than commercial banks. Their longevity since 1936 demonstrates institutional stability and deep community roots in South Fork and the surrounding region. The credit union maintains physical branch locations, offering in-person banking services unavailable through purely online institutions.

A significant limitation is the lack of current rate transparency on the BestCashCow profile—deposit and loan rates are listed as unavailable as of April 6, 2026. This suggests the credit union may not actively maintain rate information through third-party aggregators, making it necessary to contact the institution directly for current product details and pricing. Additionally, as a small regional credit union with approximately $47 million in assets, they may have more limited product offerings and service capabilities compared to larger national credit unions or banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: NCUA insured federal credit union providing deposit protection up to standard NCUA limits
Established in 1936 with 90+ years of operational history and community presence
Credit union structure typically offers lower fees and more favorable rates than commercial banks
Offers full suite of retail banking products: savings, CDs, mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and HELOCs
Physical branch locations in South Fork, PA for in-person banking services
Competitive CD rates with 1-year CDs at 4.59% APY and 5-year CDs at 4.00% APY
Federally chartered with capital of $9.80 million indicating financial stability
cons: Current deposit and loan rates unavailable on aggregator sites as of April 2026, requiring direct contact for pricing information
Very small institution ($47.19 million in assets) may mean limited product customization and fewer service options than larger competitors
Limited geographic footprint as a regional South Fork, PA-based credit union with restricted membership eligibility
No online rate information available, suggesting minimal digital marketing and limited transparency through online channels
Small asset base may limit lending capacity for larger loans or commercial products
best_for: South Fork, PA residents and surrounding communities seeking local banking relationships and in-person service
Members wanting traditional full-service banking with credit union member-ownership benefits and favorable rates
Consumers prioritizing long-established institutions with community roots and stable operational histories
Borrowers seeking mortgages, auto loans, or personal loans from a local, relationship-focused lender
diagnosis: Prr South Fork Federal Credit Union is best for residents of South Fork, Pennsylvania and surrounding areas seeking traditional, relationship-based banking from a stable, member-owned institution with 90 years of history. The main caveat is that as a very small regional credit union, you'll need to contact them directly for current rates and product details, as this information is not readily available through online aggregators, and membership eligibility may be restricted by geography or employer affiliation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
